{"code":"import json, re, pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

# load latest package versions
latest_obj = json.loads(Path(var_call_G5vTvfcZmYx9KtyVIoSIOggY).read_text())
latest_df = pd.DataFrame(latest_obj['latest'])

# load mappings to project
ppv = pd.DataFrame(json.loads(Path(var_call_KQfjQrAIGtkZPNKzyufbMGz8).read_text()))
ppv = ppv.drop_duplicates(subset=['Name','Version','ProjectName'])

# load project info strings
pi = pd.DataFrame(var_call_1CeGm9FlC25KuqVjRLZ6bxH7)

def parse_proj_info(s):
    if s is None:
        return None
    m = re.search(r'project (?:named )?([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)', s)
    proj = m.group(1) if m else None
    m2 = re.search(r'([0-9][0-9,]*) stars', s)
    stars = int(m2.group(1).replace(',','')) if m2 else None
    return proj, stars

parsed = [parse_proj_info(s) for s in pi['Project_Information'].tolist()]
pi['ProjectName'] = [p for p,_ in parsed]
pi['Stars'] = [st for _,st in parsed]
pi = pi.dropna(subset=['ProjectName','Stars'])

# join latest packages to their project mapping
j = latest_df.merge(ppv[['Name','Version','ProjectName']], on=['Name','Version'], how='inner')
# join to project stars
j = j.merge(pi[['ProjectName','Stars']], on='ProjectName', how='inner')

# Some packages may map to multiple projects; take max stars per package
j2 = j.groupby(['Name','Version'], as_index=False)['Stars'].max()
# top 5
j2 = j2.sort_values(['Stars','Name'], ascending=[False, True]).head(5)

lines = []
for _,r in j2.iterrows():
    lines.append(f"{r['Name']}@{r['Version']} — {int(r['Stars'])} GitHub stars")
answer = "\n".join(lines) if lines else "No matching NPM packages with GitHub star information found."
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'answer': answer}))"}
